key_insight: MARLIN can infer likely molecular structures directly from tandem mass spectra without first knowing the molecular formula, removing a major bottleneck in de novo metabolite identification.
clinical_relevance: By improving identification of unknown small molecules, this could support biomarker discovery, natural product research, and therapeutic discovery workflows where standard libraries fail.
method: Self-supervised encoder predicts a molecular fingerprint from raw MS/MS peaks.
Block-diffusion language model generates candidate structures using the fingerprint plus measured precursor mass.
A mass-shell constraint and exact ppm mass matching keep candidates consistent with the observed mass, while a diversity mechanism reduces mode collapse.
evidence_signal: On the NPLIB1 benchmark, MARLIN was the strongest formula-free method across exact-match accuracy, structural distance, and fingerprint similarity, and it recovered the correct molecular formula about as often as a dedicated predictor.
watch_item: Performance is benchmark-based, so real-world utility will depend on robustness to noisy spectra, diverse instruments, and truly novel chemistries outside the evaluation set.
paper_title: MARLIN: De Novo Molecular Structure Elucidation from Tandem Mass Spectra without a Ground-Truth Formula
